title: T-072 — Docs gap: supplier_specs Phase 1 upload/view/review implementation brief
prompt:
# T-072 — Docs gap: supplier_specs Phase 1 upload/view/review implementation brief

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.5, §15A.3, §15A.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Self-contained Phase 1 supplier_specs upload/view/review brief.

The brief must be implementable by later schema/API/UI tasks without rediscovery. Include exact candidate routes, server actions, fields, validation states, permissions, events and test expectations. Mark supplier portal/external negotiation as Phase 2/out of scope.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- design/specs/TEC-supplier-specs-phase1.md [create]

## Out of scope
- Implementing API routes
- Implementing UI components
- Supplier portal Phase 2

## Acceptance criteria
1. Brief states supplier_specs is Phase 1 internal Technical-maintained master data and distinct from customer_spec and factory_specs/internal_product_spec.
2. Brief defines upload/view/review flow including document metadata, status transitions, Technical approval/rejection, audit evidence and permissions.
3. Brief says PO/TO actual deviations create review tasks/events/non_conformance.requested and do not silently overwrite approved supplier_specs or BOM cost_per_kg.
4. Brief defines RM usability hooks: approved supplier, active approved non-expired supplier_spec, allergen compatibility, active item, cost/spec review not blocked, and QC/release status where configured.
5. Brief names candidate API routes/actions and UI states but does not implement app code.

## Test strategy
- Manual docs review.
- No automated tests for docs.

## Risk red lines
- Do not invent a supplier portal implementation for Phase 1.
- Do not implement app code in this docs task.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
